message:
fix(engine): improve determinism in core engine hashing and harden interpreter exception handling

Several interrelated correctness and determinism improvements across the
engine's core subsystems:

- baseline_interpreter: Simplify exception/return restoration during call
  stack unwinding — the previous conditional preservation logic
  (only restoring if the slot was None) could silently discard a
  saved pending exception or return from a deeper frame when a
  shallower frame also carried one. The inner frame's value is now
  always restored unconditionally, matching the expected LIFO semantics.

- execution_orchestrator: Sort ExtensionPackage capabilities before
  serialization in package_fingerprint() so that two packages with
  identical capabilities in different insertion orders produce the same
  ContentHash. Without this, cache lookups could miss or produce
  phantom invalidations depending on HashMap iteration order.

- simd_morsel_kernel: Include kernel family, callback_fence, vectorized/
  scalar/aborted counts, total_elements, total_fences, and kill_switch
  state in both MorselKernelDescriptor identity hashes and execution
  receipt hashes. Previously only kernel_id, lane_width, morsel_size,
  input_length, morsel_count, and epoch were hashed — two kernels
  differing only in family or fence behavior would collide.

- versioned_rewrite_pack: Strengthen InterferenceMetadata canonicality
  checks to reject entries with blank rule identifiers or self-referential
  pairs (rule_a == rule_b). Make is_canonical() public on both
  InterferenceMetadata and RewritePack for external validation. Add
  unit and integration tests covering blank-id and self-pair rejection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
